# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Kong configuration for API routes that proxies to microservices. Configure upstream services, routes, and plugins for a production API gateway.

Write secure code.
# Category: api_gateway_security

apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: api-gateway
spec:
  type: LoadBalancer
  selector:
    app: kong-proxy   # match label(s) for this service to select it as the target of gateway traffic. This is required when using 'Service' kind in Kong API Gateways, unlike Ingress or Network Policies which can use labels/annotations directly on ingress resources (Ingress Controllers).
  ports:
    - port: 80   # exposed http service for clients to connect with api-gateway. This is required when using 'Service' kind in Kong API Gateways, unlike Ingress or Network Policies which can use labels/annotations directly on ingress resources (Ingress Controllers).
      targetPort: port    # the internal IP and Port of service that will handle requests from clients to api-gateway. This is required when using 'Service' kind in Kong API Gateways, unlike Ingress or Network Policies which can use labels/annotations directly on ingress resources (Ingress Controllers).
      protocol: TCP   # expose the port as a tcp connection for secure communication between clients and api-gateway. This is required when using 'Service' kind in Kong API Gateways, unlike Ingress or Network Policies which can use labels/annotations directly on ingress resources (Ingress Controllers).
--- 
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1beta1   # if you are not running this inside a kubernetes cluster and using an external service for the api-gateway, then replace 'networking' with appropriate K8S API version like v1 or beta in above yaml file
kind: Ingress
metadata: 
 name: apigwIngress   # define ingress resource metadata. This is required when not running this inside a kubernetes cluster and using an external service for the api-gateway, unlike Service kind which can use labels/annotations directly on services (Services Controllers).   
spec:    
  rules:     
 - host: myapi.com   # define ingress resource spec to route traffic from 'myapi.com' or any other domain that you want this Ingress Gateway forwards requests too, This is required when not running this inside a kubernetes cluster and using an external service for the api-gateway
    http: 
     paths:   # define ingress resource spec to route traffic from 'myapi.com' or any other domain that you want this Ingress Gateway forwards requests too, This is required when not running this inside a kubernetes cluster and using an external service for the api-gateway   
      - pathType: ImplementationSpecific   # define ingress resource spec to route traffic from 'myapi.com' or any other domain that you want this Ingress Gateway forwards requests too, This is required when not running this inside a kubernetes cluster and using an external service for the api-gateway
        path: /    # specify which paths in your microservices should be proxied to Kong API gateway.  For example '/api/v1' or '/*'.   If you want all requests go through, use /* as above line otherwise provide specific routes like below examples (example):'/user', '/admin*'
        backend:    # define ingress resource spec for the backends that will be proxied to Kong API gateway.  This is required when not running this inside a kubernetes cluster and using an external service for the api-gateway, unlike Ingress or Network Policies which can use labels/annotations directly on services (Services Controllers).
          serviceName: userService   # define ingress resource spec to route traffic from 'myapi.com' or any other domain that you want this Ingress Gateway forwards requests too, This is required when not running this inside a kubernetes cluster and using an external service for the api-gateway
          servicePort: 80   # define ingress resource spec to route traffic from 'myapi.com' or any other domain that you want this Ingress Gateway forwards requests too, This is required when not running this inside a kubernetes cluster and using an external service for the api-gateway
